response: In C# you can measure the time it takes to send a UDP packet by using a stopwatch before and after sending the packet. This will give you the time taken to send the packet from your machine to the network. However this method only measures the time on the sending side and does not account for the time it takes for the packet to reach the server. To get a one way measurement you would need to record the time before sending and then calculate the duration. This approach does not involve any round trip or response from the server.
